en: I do believe our bowels are quite charming.

And while we're in a time where many people are thinking about what new superfood smoothie to make or if gluten is maybe bad for them, actually, hardly anyone seems to care about the organ where this happens.
ko: 새로운 슈퍼푸드 스무디나 글루텐 걱정은 하면서 정작 그걸 처리하는 장기에는 관심이 없죠.

en: So it turns out we don't only have this outer sphincter, we also have an inner sphincter muscle.
ko: 알고 보면 바깥쪽 괄약근만 있는 게 아니라 안쪽 괄약근도 있어요.

en: So what happens is when there are leftovers from digestion, they're being delivered to the inner one first.
ko: 소화 잔여물이 생기면 먼저 안쪽 괄약근으로 전달돼요.

en: And this inner one will open in a reflex and let through a little bit for testing. There are sensory cells that will analyze what has been delivered.
ko: 안쪽 괄약근이 반사적으로 열려서 조금 통과시키고, 감각 세포가 뭐가 왔는지 분석해요.

en: And this is the moment when our brain knows, oh, I have to go to the toilet.
ko: 이때 뇌가 알죠, 아 화장실 가야겠다.

en: It will mediate with our surroundings, and it will say something like, so I checked, we are at this TEDx conference.
ko: 뇌가 주변 상황을 파악해요. 확인해봤는데 지금 TEDx 컨퍼런스야.

en: Gaseous, maybe, if you're sitting on the sides and you know you can pull it off silently.
ko: 가스라면 모를까, 옆자리에 앉아서 소리 없이 할 수 있다면.

en: But solid, maybe later. And they put it back in a waiting line for other times, like, for example, when we're at home, sitting on the couch, we have nothing better to do, we are free to go.
ko: 고체면 나중에. 대기열에 다시 넣어놔요. 집에서 소파에 앉아 할 일 없을 때 자유롭게 가면 되니까.

en: I had some newfound respect for that nice inner sphincter dude.
ko: 착한 안쪽 괄약근 녀석에게 새삼 존경심이 생겼어요.

en: And I used to not be a great fan of public restrooms, but now I can go anywhere, because I consider it more when that inner muscle puts a suggestion on my daily agenda.
ko: 공중화장실을 안 좋아했는데 이제는 어디든 갈 수 있어요. 안쪽 근육이 제안하면 더 신중하게 생각하거든요.
